subject: ENG
course_description: Slavery is not an unchanging systems rooted in the past. We will examine the nature and diversity of slavery in Africa, from 1300s to 1900s, and interrogate the significant role slavery, slave trades, racism, colonialism, and forced labor have played in shaping the African past and present.
course_notes: Slavery is not an unchanging system rooted in the distant past. It is a dynamic institution that transformed across centuries and continents. Slavery and the many slave trades that coexisted throughout Africa adapted continuously to external demand, evolving into new and varied systems of labor exploitation that left lasting marks on different societies. We will examine the nature and remarkable diversity of slavery within the African continent, spanning from the 1300s to the 1900s, and interrogate the significant roles that different trade routes, racism, colonialism, and forced labor have played in shaping both the African past and present realities. Far from being a singular, static phenomenon, slavery was a shifting web of economic, political, and social forces that tied Africa to the broader world in profoundly unequal ways. Understanding this complexity is essential to grasping how its lasting consequences continue to echo across African societies today.
